segments.text: So, today we are going to do lecture 10. Let me recall things we have done so far. We talked
about the concept of a inverse of a matrix. So, we said a matrix has an inverse called
m if by multiplying m on the either side produces me the identity matrix. And the
concept of inverse was only for square matrices. So, that we can have both the left inverse
and the right inverse. The way we found inverse was basically solving these three systems at the
same time. So, we said if I can let us say if we are in a 3 by 3 situation if we can solve these
three systems and they all of them are consistent then we know that the inverse
will exist. So, our strategy was to deal with this in one shot and basically start with the
matrix so called augmented matrix with A and try to row reduce it. If you are able to row
reduce it and making sure that the left hand matrix turns out to be I. So, whatever matrix
you get on the right hand side will be your inverse. This tells us a couple of things.
Let us see what does it exactly tell us. So, we want to do something called the fundamental
theorem of invertible matrices. The reason this is called a fundamental theorem is basically
it gives you various equivalent ways to understand when a matrix is inverted. So,
suppose we are given n by n matrix some field F. So, usually it will be real numbers or complex
numbers in our case then all the statements that I am going to write are equivalent. Now,
some of the statements I am going to write will not make any sense today because we
have not seen those concepts, but for the sake of completeness I am going to put them in
this list anyway. So, A is invertible it is equivalent to saying that the row reduce form
of the row reduce form of A is actually the identity matrix. Now, why this result is true?
Remember we said when we are going to deal with inverses of a matrix then if I start
with a matrix A if I can reduce it to I whatever I get on the right hand side will
be my inverse of the matrix. So, that means the row reduce form of A must be I. So, this thing
we kind of get out of the definition itself. What else we can say if the row reduce form
is I what can I say about the rank of my matrix A? Well if the row reduce form is I that means
all the rows and hence all the columns are pivots. So, that means it is a full set of
pivots. So, the rank of my matrix must be N. So, if I have N vectors as columns of your N rows
as rows of A all of them are pivot rows and pivot columns. What else can I say? Let us see
A is invertible we have the row reduce form we have the rank taken care of. Let us talk
about the linear independence. If the rank of my matrix is N then all the columns are
pivot columns. So, this means that the columns of A are linearly independent. In fact, we will see
another way to check this fact very soon. Since all the rows are pivot rows. So, this
also tells me that the rows of my matrix rows of A are also linearly independent. So,
what we are saying is if any of these areas can be fulfilled by our matrix then all the
things can be cleaned. For example, if I create a matrix whose rows are linearly independent and
suppose I have a square matrix then I can just because of this fact is true we can say that
the matrix must be invertible. So, these are all equivalent criterias. So, in mathematics
we have this acronym which is not very very catchy which says the following are equivalent.
So, that means these are all biconditional statements only the statements one of them
is true then all of them would be true. Let us think about the homogeneous system.
Can you say something about the homogeneous system A x equal to 0. What can you say about
the homogeneous system? Well let us see if the columns of A are linearly independent that means
the only linear combination of these columns which can produce me a zero vector is the
combination. So, I can say that homogeneous system has only the zero solution. Now, once
the homogeneous system has only the zero solution that means we can now talk about
any system of equations even a non-homogeneous one. So, for example, A x is equal to b
because if there is no homogeneous component that means there is no free variable and
that means the number of solutions for any consistent system will be unique.
Now, hidden in this statement is something that one should see will have a unique solution
for all b. So, in some sense I am claiming two things in one go. I am saying that
A x is equal to b is not only consistent for every b but it is also going to have
a unique solution. So, how do I know that it is consistent for every b that comes from
the fact that the rank of my matrix is n. So, all the pivots are in the coefficient matrix.
So, that means that no pivot will enter the right hand side of my augmented matrix
that guarantees me that I will have a unique solution for every b. Based on this I can
also make the following claim. Remember A x is equal to b is consistent if and only if
b can be represented as a linear combination of columns of A. So, in that case now I can come
out and say that the span of the columns of A is actually entire R n. So, if I have an
invertible matrix its columns will span the entire space which means all such systems are
solvable and they will have a unique solution. All these things can be written as all these
that the columns or the rows will form a basis. Now right now we are not familiar with the word
basis and as I mentioned before I started writing this result that there are some terms
which we are not familiar with but I want to have them under one umbrella so that we have
all the things under this one theorem. These things we will cover later on when we
understand the term basis. But let me just keep on increasing this list to include some
future things also and then we will focus on some of the results in a little more detail.
Now what else can I claim out of what we have already done so far. There is one more thing
which I am going to write again it may not make sense at this moment. The determinant of
the matrix A so we may not know what determinant is right now. The determinant of the matrix A
would be not zero if the if A is invertible. Then there is one more thing that A can written
as the product of matrices. So, there are three terms out here which we do not perhaps
do not understand the meaning of. The basis the determinant and elementary matrices
as I said we will pick up one at a time. But let me first focus on some of the things which
we are more familiar with. So, let us look at a system of equation Ax is equal to b.
Let us start with Ax is equal to zero first. So, let us consider this guy. If I know that
A is a vertical which is what we are seeing over there. If A has an inverse then what we
can do is we can come here and multiply both sides by my matrix M. This thing will give
me identity and this will give me the zero vector identity times the x vector is zero.
So, this tells me that the homogeneous system will have only one solution namely the zero
solution. In some sense this is another corroboration of this fact that the homogeneous
system has only the zero solution. Since the homogeneous system has only the zero
solution that means the columns of A must be linearly independent. And since you only
have zero solution there is no free variable hence the rank must be M. So, you are
getting all these things out of some idea like this. So, you can prove it using this
way of thinking as well. This another thing which we can say at this stage again we are
assuming A has an inverse. So, again I will multiply both the sides by M this becomes
identity. So, I can say x is M times B and this is going to be the unique solution.
Notice I did not put any condition on B. So, that means this system is solvable for every
B as long as I am guaranteeing the existence of M which kind of further tells us that Ax is
equal to B will have a unique solution for every B and this is the unique solution for a given B.
So, these are the reasons why we want to study invertible matrices and we shall see that these
statements will turn out to be very very useful as we go along. So, keep these statements in
mind they are very important and we will be using them back and forth back and forth.
Let us try let us see if we can try to answer a simple problem based on things we have seen so
far. So, let us just first understand the concept of finding a solution. So, here is a problem that
I would like to go for suppose I know that this guy has solution 1 0 0 it has a solution
let me call that solution as x 1 the vector x 1. So, if I plug in the vector x 1 here
that satisfies this equation. Likewise suppose I know that the system Ax is equal to 0 1 0
has a solution say x 2 and let us say the system Ax is equal to 0 0 1 has a solution x 3.
So, I am assuming that is a 3 by 3 matrix with this piece of information given to us.
So, question for us is what is the solution of Ax is equal to say so what can I say about
the solution of Ax is equal to 1 E pi. Now, the moment I know that these three systems
are consistent and their solutions are this I know that my matrix is invertible,
but wait a second I am not even given my matrix A. So, what exactly how will I how
will I be able to find what I am supposed to do. So, question is can I find the solution
of this system in terms of the given information to us. So, here is a simple
we are looking at things perhaps it can help us. What we know so far is if you look at
1 E pi can I say this is 1 times the vector 1 0 0 plus E times the vector 0 1 0 plus pi
times the vector 0 0 1. So, 1 E pi can be written as a linear combination of the right
sides that I have over here. Now, look at this guy we are saying Ax is equal to 1 0 0 has a
solution x 1. So, can I say that this thing is actually A times x 1 likewise can I say this is
A times x 2 and can I say this thing is A times x 3 because I know that x 1 x 2 x 3 the
solutions are there. So, can I now say that it will be A times x 1 plus E x 2. So, A times
that vector. So, if I multiply my matrix A with that vector then I will get 1 E pi hence we
can say that the solution of our system is the vector x 1 E x 2 plus pi x 3. The idea
this problem is to show you something which is kind of convenient for invertible matrices
just knowing the solution of three systems we are claiming that we can know the solution for
any system. So, in some sense a little bit of information is helpful for us in capturing
information for infinitely many possibilities of the right hand side and that is something which
we will keep on playing in linear algebra every now and then that there will be a bunch
of vectors which can help you understand the information about the entire space and that is
what makes it very interesting as well as beautiful. So, let us try doing a little more
about some of the equivalent statements we have seen there was a term I had used elementary
matrices question is what exactly is an elementary matrix and how do I know that it can written
as a product of elementary matrices. So, let us come to the notion of elementary matrices well
an elementary matrix is a matrix that we obtain by performing one row operation
on an identity matrix. So, any matrix that I get by performing one row operation on
an identity matrix we will call that as elementary matrix. So, let us take example
case of 2 by 2. So, suppose I have my matrix identity matrix 1 0 0 1 and suppose I perform
one row operation on it a row operation could be just like multiplying second row by say 2.
So, I will get 1 0 0 2. So, this is an elementary matrix likewise if you look at
this matrix I will start with the matrix first and then suppose I am given this matrix I claim
that this is an elementary matrix because if you start with your identity matrix question is can
I perform one row operation to get this. So, suppose I take minus 3 r 1 plus r 2 you will
see I will get that matrix. So, that means, it is an the matrix that I have got is an
elementary matrix likewise if you look at this 0 1 1 0 this is an elementary matrix because if
I will start with identity matrix and I swap rows 1 and 2 then I will get 0 1 1 0. So,
that is also an elementary matrix. So, elementary matrix can be used to represent
one row operation. Now, let us see what is the effect of an elementary matrix
what is the effect of using an elementary matrix suppose I am given a matrix A and let us say on
the left I multiply it by this elementary matrix 1 0 minus 3 1 what will happen I am assuming
is also 2 by 2 matrix. So, let us see let us give it some increase 1 0 minus 3 1 if you
on the left of A let us see what happens 1 0 9 A C gives me A this gives me B this gives me minus
3 A plus C and this gives me minus 3 B plus D you will say well. So, what look at the matrix
that we have got if I had started if I start with the matrix A and I ask you what should I
do to get this matrix 3 A plus C minus 3 B plus D in terms of row operation to W you would say
minus 3 times R 1 when added to R 2 will give me 1 will give me the matrix on the right hand
side. So, what is the effect of multiplying by an elementary matrix on the left it helps you
perform exactly one row operation on your matrix try it with other matrices also for example,
if you try multiplying it with this one the effect would be as if I have multiplied the second
row by 2 or if I multiply by this elementary matrix the effect will be swapping the rows 1 and 2
and this is where elementary matrices are useful in some sense now we can encode out
all our row operations as just matrix multiplications. So, we do not have to
really learn anything else everything can be in some sense obtained in terms of matrix
multiplication. So, let us note down this finding that we have and see how is this useful for us.
So, here is our finding. So, to perform one row operation on matrix A we just need to multiply
A on the left by an appropriate elementary matrix this is what we will use in order to do

is actually a product of elementary matrices I will use this and everything but before I really

So, there are couple of obvious properties of the inverses which you can try to prove on

matrix and you want the inverse of it, it would be simply the reciprocal of A
the reciprocal of the scalar k and multiplied by the inverse of this matrix.
Ok this is the property which we are going to looking forward to the inverses if they exist
are unique that means once you have found a matrix which acts as an inverse that is the

properties these two are obvious which you can work out the details yourself.
Regarding the uniqueness of the inverse suppose I have two inverses or two matrices claiming to
be inverses then I have to show you that they are both same this is a very typical
uniqueness proof in math you assume there are two things and then you show they are the same.
So, let me try proving the third property. So, suppose I have two matrices m 1 m 2 such that
A m 1 m 1 A is identity A m 2 is also that means they are both acting as inverses of A.
So, I my job is to show you that m 1 must be the same as m 2. So, how do we do something

I can think of I as A remember A m 2 is also I. So, I am just replacing I by A m 2 then using
the associativity property of matrix multiplication I can write this as m 1 A times m 2, but then
m 1 A is identity. So, it is identity times m 2 that means m 1 is the same as m 2. So,
if there are two matrices which were acting as inverses they are actually the same matrix.
So, the inverse is unique now regarding this fourth property if you notice this property
seems very innocent, but there is something hidden in here which you should be careful
what it is saying is if I give you matrix A and matrix B then the inverse of that
matrix would be B inverse A inverse. So, first thing is first the order has changed
then we will see why secondly this result is also kind of assuming that both A and B are
invertible then only A times B will be invertible. So, in other words this
this result is telling you that if even one of them was not invertible
then this result may not be true. So, keep that in mind.
So, let me try to deal with it over here the proving this is not that difficult because

I will not go through it myself even sort of do it as a practice,
but the things which are important which we want to make a note of is as follows. So,
this result is assuming that both A and B are invertible then the product will be invertible.
So, question now is is it possible that one of them is not invertible maybe A or maybe
B and is and yet the product could be invertible. So, this is the more important

That means, even if one of them is not invertible can the product be invertible can it happen that
we can have maybe perhaps a different formula for A times B in that situation or could we

So, this is the question we want to answer and ask ourselves can the product be invertible

I will come back to the elementary matrices do not worry about it. It says that we have

Now, let us try to answer this question I will at least try to answer it with one

we say about B and whole lot of things we can say. For example, we can say remember
the fundamental theorem of invertible matrices if one of them does not hold true then none

the columns of B the rows of B cannot be independent so and so forth. In particular,

If it is not invertible then that means the homogeneous system must have a non-trivial
solution let us call it as x0. That means apart from the zero solution it must have a
something which is a non-zero solution. But then if you look at this guy AB times x0
would you agree this can be thought of as A times B x0 which is A times 0 which is 0.
So, this implies that if you look at this homogeneous system non-trivial this means

if B fails to be invertible then B has a non-trivial solution for its homogeneous system
and that same solution will work as a non-trivial solution for the
this system AB x equal to 0 and since it has a non-trivial solution by the fundamental theorem
we can say it is not invertible. So, at least we know some answer to it
that if B is not invertible it has B cannot be invertible even though A may be. So,

remember I want the row reduced echelon form. So, all my pivot entries must be 1. Now,

So, by my by the fundamental theorem of invertible matrices I know for sure that A is invertible

Let us look at this we said if you perform one row operation it can be represented by

added to row 1. Now, recall what did we say we said in order to perform the row operation

So, if I multiply my matrix A on the left by E I will get this new matrix which if you want

Then in order to reach the identity matrix I will left multiply C by the matrix G. So,
